methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $37M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
EX-Fusion, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Kazuki Matsuo and Shinsuke Fujioka, has raised $37M to date. The nuclear energy sector includes 3 public comps (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo, providing relevant but limited benchmarking context.

Stage-progression methodology applies here, using the $37M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage transitions. This yields an implied valuation of $74M. The peer-median approach is not applicable given insufficient narrow-stage peers within nuclear energy; while 8 same-sector companies exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables yields fewer than the three datapoints required for robust cross-sectional analysis. A broad sector-wide median would introduce material distortion.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $63M to $85M, centered on $74M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable method rather than triangulation across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of sufficient same-stage nuclear peers for direct comparison, the nascent stage of the private fusion sector generally, and unobserved operational metrics (burn rate, technical milestones, IP position). Further data on comparable Series B outcomes in nuclear energy and EX-Fusion's specific development trajectory would strengthen conviction in this range.
